key_insight: Peak major stress in Rubin Observatory’s M1M3 mirror can be estimated by root-sum-square (RSS) combining precomputed unit bending-mode stresses, mirroring the way bending displacements are combined.
engineering_relevance: This enables near-instant stress-margin checking and actuator-force optimization for active optics operations without running a full finite element solve each time.
method: Compute unit displacement and unit stress fields for the first 20 M1M3 bending modes with NASTRAN.
Approximate multi-mode response by RSS combination of mode-wise stress contributions.
Compare RSS-predicted peak principal stress against full NASTRAN simulations for representative mode combinations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that validation against representative multi-mode cases showed agreement within a few percent for peak major principal stress across the mirror substrate.
watch_item: RSS stress superposition is validated only for representative mode combinations, so unusual load cases or nonlinear effects may require full FE verification.
paper_title: Determining stress-based bending mode limits for the Vera C. Rubin Observatory M1M3 active mirror system
